report_name: Major Security Lapse at CISA: Contractor Exposes GovCloud Keys and Internal Credentials on Public GitHub Repository

threat_actors.name: CISA Contractor
threat_actors.description: A contractor for CISA who exposed sensitive credentials, including AWS GovCloud keys, SSH keys, and access tokens, on a public GitHub repository named 'Private-CISA'. The exposure was due to negligence, using the public repository to synchronize work files between computers.
threat_actors.goals: Synchronize work files between different computers.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: A contractor transferred 844 MB of sensitive data, including credentials and keys, to a public GitHub repository to synchronize files between work and home computers, leading to accidental exposure.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: Plaintext credentials for AWS GovCloud, SSH keys, and Artifactory access tokens were stored in files within a public GitHub repository.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials In Files
attack_patterns.description: Plaintext credentials for AWS GovCloud accounts and Artifactory access tokens were exposed within files in a public GitHub repository.

attack_patterns.name: Private Keys
attack_patterns.description: SSH keys and an RSA private key for a GitHub app were exposed in plaintext within a public GitHub repository.

tools.name: GitHub
tools.description: A public GitHub repository named 'Private-CISA' was used by a contractor to store and synchronize sensitive CISA data, leading to its exposure.

identities.name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
identities.description: The U.S. government agency whose sensitive data, including AWS GovCloud keys and internal system credentials, was exposed by a contractor on a public GitHub repository.

infrastructure.name: Public GitHub Repository "Private-CISA"
infrastructure.description: A public GitHub repository created by a contractor to sync files, which inadvertently exposed 844 MB of sensitive CISA credentials and data.

indicators.name: AWS Access Key ID Pattern Detected in Public Repository
indicators.pattern:
title: AWS Access Key ID Detected in Code Repository
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a3b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the presence of a string matching the AWS Access Key ID format (AKIA...). The presence of such keys in public code repositories is a critical security risk, as seen in the CISA contractor leak.
references:
  - https://krebsonsecurity.com/2026/05/cisa-admin-leaked-aws-govcloud-keys-on-github/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1552.001
level: high
logsource:
  product: github
detection:
  selection:
    keywords:
      - 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the pattern for AWS Access Key IDs in code, which should never be in public repositories.

indicators.name: RSA Private Key Header Detected in Public Repository
indicators.pattern:
title: RSA Private Key Detected in Code Repository
id: 9f8b7a6c-5d4e-3c2b-1a98-7654321fedcb
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the header for an RSA private key ('-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----'). Its presence in a public repository, as in the CISA contractor leak, is a critical security finding.
references:
  - https://krebsonsecurity.com/2026/05/cisa-admin-leaked-aws-govcloud-keys-on-github/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1552.004
level: critical
logsource:
  product: github
detection:
  selection:
    keywords:
      - '-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the header for an RSA private key, indicating a severe credential leak if found in a public repository.

relationships.source_name: AWS Access Key ID Pattern Detected in Public Repository
RSA Private Key Header Detected in Public Repository
